title: [lichen_l2.c:1325] lichen_l2_iface_init cannot return errors to Zephyr
description:
Contract: lichen_l2_iface_init should initialize the L2 layer.
Violation: Zephyr's net_if_api.init callback is void(*)(struct net_if*), so errors cannot be returned. The function sets iface_init_failed atomic and returns early on any failure. While this is handled correctly (all operations check iface_init_failed), it means Zephyr's network stack believes init succeeded.
Impact: Medium - The net_if will appear operational to the IPv6 stack even though L2 is broken. Packets sent to this interface will fail at lichen_l2_send() with -ENODEV, which is correct behavior but may confuse upper layers.
Mitigation: This is a Zephyr API limitation. The current approach (iface_init_failed flag) is the correct workaround. Consider using a Zephyr hook to mark interface as DOWN if init fails.
close_reason: Verified: The Zephyr API limitation is correctly documented (lines 1318-1321) and handled. All L2 entry points check iface_init_failed: lichen_l2_send (line 820) returns -ENODEV, lichen_l2_enable (line 964) returns -ENODEV, lora_rx_callback (line 1298) returns early. lichen_l2_recv is a passthrough that doesn't access state. lichen_l2_flags returns static values. The current defensive approach is correct - no code changes needed.
